item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Necropost imminent: I think a pretty vital piece of information is missing What language(s) are the "phrases in brackets" translated from?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can understand why it has a decent rating, and why even the author doesn't fully get why…
… well, that didn't make sense…
It is really imaginative and evocative. It sounds like something out of maritime myth, but it is (so far as I know) new. That being said, it has holes like a schooner with an immature SCP-1014 aboard. I just don't see these plaguing ships for an extended period without a paper trail. Yes, the Foundation clears those up, but usually not perfectly, just enough to make new reports seem ridiculous (as evidenced by all of the other maritime myths). That and the issues mentioned above.
While I am downvoting, compliments to Anaxagoras for creating something that can be this good while being this flawed! I know you don't want to rewrite it, but if you ever get really bored it might be worth a touch-up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the log at the end. Nicely evocative; not too vague and not too explained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Its reasonably creepy and dangerous without being OMG your eyeballs has explode. In other words it has a nice balance to it and reminds me a little of the Thing in terms of the mimicry angle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is this as highly rated as it is? I mean, I'm saying this as the author. I don't really have much of a hook, and the things just sink ships. Dunno, I'm not all that thrilled with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually like that idea. That said, not sure if I'll bother editing this one. I'm not thrilled with it, but I don't think that would address the issues I have with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I could see them mimicing whale song to cause them to beach. Then attaching themselves to the animal, or maybe somehow inducing the whale to swallow them strengthening the Jonah references, and the life-cycle proceeds.

Of course, this would be a rather signifigant departure from the way it's written now. For one thing, the wood rotting effect would have to be expanded to organic material to let them "spore" from a whale host.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've gotta agree with Rhett on this one - reasonably strong concept, but the writeup needs some work for phrasing, flow, and tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who says they existed before the coming of wooden sailing ships?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what they used to mimic before the coming of wooden sailing ships? Or did they have to mimic anything?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It's imaginative. +1'ed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this one, so much so I made a picture for it
No Frills
Zoom and Stuff

You can use it if you want

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is part of the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd think that the Foundation would notice that "Let the depths rise up and sweep over the shores, rendering the works of man to dust" is said in two different languages, since it suggests that something more than mere mimicry might be going on. I mean, it's possible that the species learned it from one human, repeated it to a different humans, one of those humans translated it out loud, and it then learned the translation. But still, "this means something more" isn't a possibility the foundation would ignore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Correct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 24 hour clock, I'd wager.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What do the numbers in the square brackets mean in the last log? Years? How'd they find these out?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
HERE YA GO

Just kidding. I'll see what I can do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could you elaborate? Also, I'd appreciate your assistance in finding an image for this, if you don't mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wrote most of this after 2:00 AM, so that's not surprising. Could you please point out where?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this, good work. That said there's some pretty awkward phrasing in places, I recommend going over it again and rewording some stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know. Unless they clustered in the past, this kind of reproduction would still be kinda cruddy. I mean, in the good times even bacteria exchange genetic material.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're dying out. Evolution ain't exactly on their side right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Self-fertilization is a terrible way to go about the reproductive process. Otherwise, upvoted, I like this.
